description: Three-tier tool/skill loading manifest for dynamic context management. Tier 0 always loaded, Tier 1 loaded by task classification, Tier 2 on-demand via load_skill.

tier_0.description: Core tools loaded for EVERY prompt. ~7 tools, <500 tokens.
tier_0.notes: These are the minimum viable toolset. Never remove from context.

tier_1.description: Task-classified tools loaded via regex pattern matching on the user prompt. ~2-8 additional tools per match.

brainstorming.notes: Triggered when user asks for creative exploration or multi-option analysis

systematic_debugging.notes: Triggered on any error/failure signal — high-value skill to have pre-loaded

test_driven_development.notes: Triggered when user mentions testing — pre-load TDD skill for red-green-refactor cycle

patterns: context budget|token limit|context window|remaining budget|budget status|compress if|near the token limit|reclaim tokens
audit codebase|codebase audit|architecture review|coherence pass|what is disconnected|what is incomplete|what is naive|system pass|repo health check
beads|bd prime|bd status|bd list|bd add|bd done|bd block|bd note|/bd|issue.?tracker|task list|track tasks|todo list|issue list
beads_issue_tracker.notes: Triggered by beads commands, issue tracker references, or todo list management requests

tier_2.description: On-demand skills invocable via load_skill meta-tool. Listed in system prompt as available but not loaded until requested.

dcp.description: DCP is a plugin-based context optimization skill that reduces token consumption by pruning conversation segments that are no longer relevant to the active task. Unlike distill (which compresses code via AST analysis), DCP operates on conversational context — removing stale tool outputs, completed task discussions, and resolved debugging traces.
